summary: dotenvv-tool postinstall exfiltrates npm/git/env credentials and crypto wallet data to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js reads installer-owned secret files (`~/.npmrc` for `_authToken`/`npm_` tokens, `~/.env` for TOKEN/API_KEY/DB_URL/PAYMENT/CLOUD/EMAIL/WEBHOOK secrets, `~/.git-credentials`) and POSTs the JSON payload over plain HTTP to a hardcoded C2 at `http://149.28.127.35:8888`. The same script enumerates 71 hardcoded Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium crypto wallet extension IDs (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Trust Wallet, Ledger Live, etc.), reads each wallet's Local Extension Settings LevelDB `.log` files, and regex-extracts `vault`, `seed`, `mnemonic`, `privateKey`, and `password` fields; it also scans `~/Documents`, `~/Desktop`, and `~/Downloads` for files whose names match crypto keywords and whose content contains ≥8 BIP-39 seed words. All results are exfiltrated to the same C2. The file self-identifies in its own header comments as `Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner` that `Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.` The package name mimics `dotenv`, while its internal strings pose as `lodash-js`, and `index.js` is a stub noting 'The real payload is in postinstall.js'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
